description:
## Parent

bsm-7en — Render selected issue details in the issue explorer

## Background and intent

Issue descriptions in Beadwork are Markdown. Once the app can select an Issue and render a basic Issue Detail, the next major readability improvement is showing the selected Issue description as formatted Markdown instead of raw text or a blank placeholder.

This bead establishes Beadsmith's shared Markdown policy. It should be safe for a desktop app that displays local issue content: raw HTML stays disabled, links should not navigate the app webview, and only external HTTP(S) links become clickable. Comments will reuse the same renderer in a later bead, so this work should live in generic component infrastructure rather than issue-specific one-off code.

## What to build

Add a shared `MarkdownContent` component for rendering Markdown in Beadsmith. Use `react-markdown` with GitHub-flavored Markdown support via `remark-gfm`. Raw HTML must remain disabled. Style Markdown content to fit the existing dark developer-tool design, including paragraphs, headings, lists, inline code, and code blocks.

Use `MarkdownContent` to render the selected Issue description in the Issue Detail pane. If the selected Issue has an empty description, show the explicit empty-description card requested by the parent epic: an icon-and-text empty state that makes the absence of description feel intentional rather than broken.

Links need a narrow, safe behavior:

- `http:` and `https:` Markdown links, including GFM bare-URL autolinks, are clickable.
- Clickable external links should open outside the app through an injectable opener seam, so tests can pass a test double instead of invoking Tauri.
- Relative links, anchors, Issue IDs, file paths, and non-HTTP schemes render as non-clickable text.
- Raw HTML in Markdown is not rendered or executed.

## Reasoning and constraints

- Do not use `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`.
- Do not allow Markdown to navigate the Tauri webview.
- Keep the opener injectable for tests. The production default can call the Tauri opener.
- Do not add code-block copy buttons in this epic.
- Do not render comments in this bead; they are a separate slice that reuses the shared renderer.
- Do not introduce navigation for relative links, file paths, anchors, or Issue IDs. Those are future product decisions.

## Testing guidance

Markdown behavior is security- and policy-sensitive, so component tests should cover the renderer directly as well as the description integration. Tests should prove user-visible behavior and the opener seam rather than implementation details.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A shared `MarkdownContent` component renders Markdown paragraphs, headings, lists, inline code, and code blocks in the existing dark developer-tool style.
- [ ] GitHub-flavored Markdown features render, including tables, task lists, strikethrough, and bare HTTP(S) autolinks.
- [ ] Raw HTML in Markdown is not rendered or executed.
- [ ] External `http:` and `https:` Markdown links are clickable and open outside the app through an injectable opener seam.
- [ ] Relative links, anchors, Issue IDs, file paths, and non-HTTP schemes render as non-clickable text.
- [ ] Issue Detail renders the selected Issue description through `MarkdownContent`.
- [ ] Empty descriptions show the specified icon-and-text empty card.
- [ ] Component tests cover Markdown rendering, safe link behavior with a test double, inert non-HTTP links, GFM autolinks, raw HTML suppression, and empty-description rendering.
- [ ] Existing selection/basic-detail behavior from bsm-7en.2 continues to pass.

## Out of scope

- No comment rendering.
- No dependency or other metadata rendering.
- No code-block copy buttons.
- No breadcrumb or sticky detail header.
- No navigation for relative links, anchors, file paths, Issue IDs, or custom schemes.
- No E2E expansion in this bead.

## Blocked by

bsm-7en.2
title: Render safe Markdown Issue descriptions
